Title: User successfully logs in
Test Steps:
1. User enters their email and password.
2. System checks if the user exists in the database.
3. System verifies the password using bcrypt.
4. System generates a JWT token.
5. System revokes any existing tokens for the user.
6. System inserts a new token into the database.
7. System sends the JWT token to the client.
Expected Result: User is logged in and receives a valid JWT token.

Title: User fails to log in due to incorrect credentials
Test Steps:
1. User enters their email and password.
2. System checks if the user exists in the database.
3. System verifies the password using bcrypt.
4. System returns an error message.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating incorrect credentials.

Title: User fails to log in due to non-existent user
Test Steps:
1. User enters their email and password.
2. System checks if the user exists in the database.
3. System returns an error message.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating the user does not exist.

Title: Coordinator successfully creates an emergency
Test Steps:
1. Coordinator enters the emergency details.
2. System checks if the details are complete.
3. System creates the emergency.
Expected Result: Emergency is created successfully.

Title: Coordinator fails to create an emergency due to incomplete details
Test Steps:
1. Coordinator enters the emergency details.
2. System checks if the details are complete.
3. System prompts to update the details.
Expected Result: Coordinator is prompted to update the missing details.

Title: Coordinator fails to create an emergency due to unauthenticated access
Test Steps:
1. Coordinator tries to create an emergency.
2. System returns an error message.
Expected Result: Coordinator receives an error message indicating unauthenticated access.

Title: Coordinator successfully removes a volunteer
Preconditions: Coordinator is authenticated and a volunteer is assigned.
Test Steps:
1. Coordinator clicks "Visualize emergency".
2. System checks if the coordinator is authenticated.
3. System checks if a volunteer is assigned.
4. System removes the volunteer.
Expected Result: Volunteer is removed successfully.

Title: Coordinator fails to remove a volunteer due to unauthenticated access
Test Steps:
1. Coordinator tries to remove a volunteer.
2. System returns an error message.
Expected Result: Coordinator receives an error message indicating unauthenticated access.

Title: Coordinator fails to remove a volunteer due to no volunteer assigned
Preconditions: Coordinator is authenticated but no volunteer is assigned.
Test Steps:
1. Coordinator tries to remove a volunteer.
2. System returns an error message.
Expected Result: Coordinator receives an error message indicating no volunteer is assigned.

Title: Client successfully creates a new task
Test Data:
- Name: "New Task"
- Description: "This is a new task"
- Region: "Lombardy"
- Province: "Milan"
Test Steps:
1. Client sends a POST request to /nuovoCompito.
2. Server inserts the new task into the database.
3. Server returns a success message.
Expected Result: New task is created successfully.

Title: Client fails to create a new task due to invalid input
Test Data:
- Name: ""
- Description: "This is a new task"
- Region: "Lombardy"
- Province: "Milan"
Test Steps:
1. Client sends a POST request to /nuovoCompito.
2. Server returns an error message.
Expected Result: Client receives an error message indicating invalid input.

Title: Client successfully terminates an emergency
Preconditions: Emergency exists and is assigned to a volunteer.
Test Steps:
1. Client sends a POST request to /terminaEmergenza.
2. Server verifies the token.
3. Database updates the status of the emergency.
4. Server returns a success message.
Expected Result: Emergency is terminated successfully.

Title: Client fails to terminate an emergency due to invalid token
Preconditions: Emergency exists and is assigned to a volunteer.
Test Steps:
1. Client sends a POST request to /terminaEmergenza.
2. Server returns an error message.
Expected Result: Client receives an error message indicating invalid token.

Title: Client fails to terminate an emergency due to no assigned volunteer
Preconditions: Emergency exists but no volunteer is assigned.
Test Steps:
1. Client sends a POST request to /terminaEmergenza.
2. Server returns an error message.
Expected Result: Client receives an error message indicating no volunteer is assigned.

Title: Authenticated user successfully visualizes emergencies
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Visualizza emergenze".
2. System checks if emergencies are available.
3. System displays the available emergencies.
Expected Result: User sees a list of available emergencies.

Title: Unauthenticated user fails to visualize emergencies
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Visualizza emergenze".
2. System returns an error message.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating unauthenticated access.

Title: No emergencies available
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Visualizza emergenze".
2. System checks if emergencies are available.
3. System displays a message indicating no emergencies are available.
Expected Result: User sees a message indicating no emergencies are available.
